content.problemStatement:
You are given an 8×88\times 88×8 chessboard. Your enemy king is at point (X,Y)(X,Y)(X,Y) (1≤X,Y≤8)(1 \leq X,Y \leq 8)(1≤X,Y≤8).

You need to place some number of queen pieces on the chessboard such that the following conditions are satisfied:


The king should **not** be under attack by any of the queens that you have placed.
The king should **not** be able to make any move.
Note that a king can move one unit in any of the 888 direction while a queen can attack horizontally, vertically as well as diagonally.

Find the **minimum** number of queens required to satisfy the given conditions.
Print the chessboard where 000 denotes empty cells, 111 denotes the king, and 222 denotes the queen(s) placed by you in some position(s).

In case of multiple answers, print any.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers AAA and BBB — the coordinates of the king.


Output Format
For each test case, output a 8×88\times 88×8 grid consisting of King, Queen(s) and blank cells.


Print the chessboard in 888 lines where each line has 888 space-separated integers where 000 denotes empty cells, 111 denotes the king, and 222 denotes the queen(s) placed by you in some position(s).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can place 222 queens at (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) and (3,3)(3, 3)(3,3) respectively. Thus, the king at (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) has no safe squares to go to and is not under attack.
